creator: martin@waschbuesch.de
text:
(In reply to odhiambo@gmail.com from comment #21)
Reading this PR, I am confused now.

How does switching from --enable-strip to --disable-strip relate to the presence of stale libraries?

Is the switch to --disable-strip unneccessary once the stale libraries are removed?

Or are (were) there two different issues?

And out of curiosity: Are there side-effects to --disable-strip (I would assume the binaries are larger if all the debug symbols are left in)?

The number of workers I have running in order to facillitate a large number of EAS clients would make that a non-trivial issue, if it were the case...
